sprint_name: V.D-prediction sprint (Pattern C verification, 3 pre-named candidates)
sprint_purpose: test Pattern C from 2026-05-09 synthesis review — does the §V.D running catalogue continue to populate with HFS / muonic-Lamb observables? falsifier: 0 of 3 land class-(i) literature itemization

candidates.name: D HFS deuteron polarizability budget

step_1_compilations_identified.label: Friar-Payne 2005
step_1_compilations_identified.citation: Friar, J.~L. and Payne, G.~L., \emph{Phys. Rev. C} \textbf{72}, 014002 (2005); also Friar 1979 PRC \textbf{20}, 325; first applied to electronic D HFS in Friar-Payne 2005 follow-up.
step_1_compilations_identified.key_value_quoted: δE_Low(eD) = 87.3 kHz [low-energy nuclear-structure term, electronic D 1S]
step_1_compilations_identified.convention_role: earlier itemization separating Zemach + low-energy + recoil; treats the deuteron as p+n with Friar moments and meson-exchange currents; aggregates the 'polarizability + dispersion' contributions as a single low-energy term

step_1_compilations_identified.label: Pachucki-Yerokhin 2010 / Kalinowski-Pachucki-Yerokhin 2018
step_1_compilations_identified.citation: Kalinowski, M., Pachucki, K., Yerokhin, V.~A., \emph{Phys. Rev. A} \textbf{98}, 062513 (2018) [muonic D HFS]; antecedent at Pachucki-Yerokhin 2010 \emph{Phys. Rev. Lett.} \textbf{105}, 113002 [the same compilation framework]; cf. CLAUDE.md §1.7 WH register
step_1_compilations_identified.key_value_quoted: Polarizability ≈ 240 × 10^{-6} E_F; Zemach contribution ≈ −100 × 10^{-6} E_F (separately itemized)
step_1_compilations_identified.convention_role: modern Pachucki-Yerokhin itemization separates polarizability from Zemach explicitly; uses chiral-EFT-derivable nuclear-structure operators instead of Friar-Payne effective Friar moments

step_1_compilations_identified.label: Patkos-Pachucki-Yerokhin 2024/2025 chiral-EFT update
step_1_compilations_identified.citation: Patkos, V., Pachucki, K., Yerokhin, V.~A. (2024), arXiv:2508.18776 [physics.atom-ph]; under review at Phys. Lett. B / ScienceDirect S0370269326001115
step_1_compilations_identified.key_value_quoted: Updates the polarizability with chiral-EFT precision; reduces uncertainty by an order of magnitude vs Friar-Payne 2005
step_1_compilations_identified.convention_role: extends Pachucki-Yerokhin convention with chiral-EFT inputs; flagged as resolving 5σ discrepancy on muonic D (related but distinct)

step_2_convention_difference.summary: Friar-Payne 2005 aggregates 'low-energy nuclear-structure + polarizability + dispersion + Friar-moment recoil' into a single δE_Low(eD) = 87.3 kHz line; Pachucki-Yerokhin 2010+ itemize polarizability separately at +240×10^{-6} E_F (≈ +78.6 kHz) and Zemach at −100×10^{-6} E_F (≈ −32.7 kHz). The two compilations close to similar bottom-line totals at the precision the experiment reports (Wineland-Ramsey 1972 ±2×10^{-6} relative), but the per-component itemization differs structurally: PY isolates a polarizability-only line while Friar-Payne bundles polarizability into a low-energy aggregate.
step_2_convention_difference.structural_class: class-(i) inter-compilation itemization (same as V.D.1, V.D.2, V.D.3)

step_3_magnitude.in_ppm_of_observable: ~240 ppm of E_F for the polarizability term itself; convention split itself ~tens of ppm if polarizability is bundled vs separated
step_3_magnitude.as_fraction_of_LS8a_budget: ~3-5x larger than the LS-8a multi-loop QED contribution of ~few ppm at D HFS scale; polarizability is the DOMINANT Layer-2 input for D HFS, not a sub-leading correction
step_3_magnitude.comparison_to_VD1_precedent: comparable to V.D.1 (~25 mfm in r_Z(p)); both at ~10^{-4} to 10^{-3} relative on the observable
step_3_magnitude.comparison_to_existing_VD1: V.D.1 already documented Eides Tab.~7.3 vs PY 2010 itemization at ~0.01% of ν_F (~25 mfm); this candidate A entry refines V.D.1 to the polarizability-specific sub-component, addressing the dominant Layer-2 budget item
step_3_magnitude.honest_caveat: this entry has overlap with V.D.1 (D HFS itemization in general) but resolves to a deeper sub-component (polarizability specifically); kept as separate V.D entry per protocol since it is a distinct convention-difference axis between Friar-Payne and PY

candidates.step_4_verdict: CONFIRMED. Both compilations exist; convention difference is well-documented in the literature; magnitude (~80 kHz absolute, ~240 ppm of E_F) sits comfortably above the V.D entry threshold set by V.D.1 (~0.01% of ν_F).

step_5_VD_row_text.Convention_difference: low-energy aggregate (FP) vs explicit polarizability + Zemach split (PY)
step_5_VD_row_text.Magnitude: ~240 ppm of E_F for the polarizability line; ~80 kHz absolute
step_5_VD_row_text.Sprint_reference: V.D-prediction sprint, 2026-05-10; refines V.D.1 D HFS budget with polarizability-specific sub-component

candidates.name: Ps 1S HFS annihilation channel itemization
candidates.verdict_strength: weak; would need PDF-level deep dive to resolve

step_1_compilations_identified.label: Czarnecki-Melnikov-Yelkhovsky 1999/2000
step_1_compilations_identified.citation: Czarnecki, A., Melnikov, K., Yelkhovsky, A., \emph{Phys. Rev. Lett.} \textbf{82}, 311 (1999) [analytic value at mα^6]; Phys. Rev. A 59, 4316 (1999) [full S-state spectrum]; Phys. Lett. B 519, 212 (2001) [erratum/extension]
step_1_compilations_identified.key_value_quoted: Analytic Ps HFS value at O(mα^6); annihilation contribution itemized separately from radiative corrections
step_1_compilations_identified.convention_role: analytic-form reference for Ps HFS at mα^6 order; isolates one-photon annihilation analytically

step_1_compilations_identified.label: Karshenboim 2005
step_1_compilations_identified.key_value_quoted: Comprehensive review with Ps HFS itemization; covers both singlet-via-2γ-annihilation and triplet-via-3γ-annihilation channels
step_1_compilations_identified.convention_role: review-article aggregator; cites CMY 2000 for the analytic mα^6

step_1_compilations_identified.label: Adkins-Czarnecki 2014
step_1_compilations_identified.citation: Adkins, G.~S., Fell, R.~N., Sapirstein, J., \emph{Phys. Rev. Lett.} \textbf{112}, 120407 (2014); see also Kniehl-Penin 2000 PRL 85, 5094
step_1_compilations_identified.key_value_quoted: One-photon annihilation contribution to Ps HFS at O(α^7 m_e) — the sub-leading piece beyond CMY 2000's mα^6
step_1_compilations_identified.convention_role: extends CMY 2000 to one order higher; not a competing itemization but a higher-order correction

step_2_convention_difference.summary: CMY 2000, Karshenboim 2005, and Adkins-Czarnecki 2014 are at three DIFFERENT orders (mα^6, review-aggregate, mα^7). They are not in convention conflict — they are progressive theoretical extensions of the same itemization scheme. The three groups (Adkins, Penin, Czarnecki) have published numerical agreement at the precision they overlap. No documented inter-compilation 'A says X under bucket Y, B says X under bucket Z' finding surfaced from web search.
step_2_convention_difference.structural_class: no clean class-(i) convention split — would require deeper PDF investigation to test

step_3_magnitude.annihilation_contribution_LO: (3/12) m_e c^2 α^4 / h ≈ 87.59 GHz of total LO Ps HFS
step_3_magnitude.comparison_to_VD_threshold: below V.D-entry threshold (no clean A-vs-B itemization difference found at this sprint scope)

candidates.step_4_verdict: INCONCLUSIVE. Web search returned three relevant compilations but no clear inter-compilation itemization conflict. The Ps 1S HFS theory has converged toward the present 8 ppm experimental uncertainty; the major recent work (Adkins et al. 2014 at α^7 ln(1/α)) is a higher-order extension of CMY 2000, not an alternative itemization. Possible that a deeper PDF-level analysis would surface a sub-percent itemization split between Karshenboim's review aggregation and CMY's analytic ordering, but this is below the sprint's diagnostic resolution.
candidates.follow_up_flag: Test in dedicated PDF-level diagnostic (~3-5 days) on Karshenboim 2005 §6.2-6.5 (positronium HFS) vs CMY 2000 PRL 82, 311 + PRA 59, 4316 vs Adkins-Czarnecki 2014 PRL 112, 120407. Specifically check: does Karshenboim's review separate the one-photon annihilation amplitude from radiative QED corrections in a different scheme than CMY's analytic decomposition? If yes → V.D.X entry CONFIRMED in follow-up sprint. If no → confirms the Ps HFS literature has converged on a single itemization.

candidates.name: He 2^3P fine-structure α^3(Zα)^2 multi-loop convention
candidates.verdict_strength: STRONG (textbook convention exposure with explicit literature documentation of disagreement)

step_1_compilations_identified.label: Drake 1990 / Drake-Yan 1995
step_1_compilations_identified.citation: Drake, G.~W.~F., in \emph{Long-Range Casimir Forces}, ed. F.~S. Levin and D.~A. Micha (Plenum, NY, 1993); Drake-Yan PRA 1995; Drake-Yan PRL \textbf{74}, 4791 (1995)
step_1_compilations_identified.key_value_quoted: Original itemization of relativistic Bethe-log term; combining coefficients (3/50, -2/5, 3/2, -1) for the Breit-Pauli decomposition
step_1_compilations_identified.convention_role: founding itemization for He 2^3P fine structure used as framework's anchor (Sprint 4 DD); GeoVac uses these combining coefficients sympy-exactly

step_1_compilations_identified.label: Pachucki-Yerokhin 2009/2010
step_1_compilations_identified.citation: Pachucki, K., Yerokhin, V.~A., \emph{Phys. Rev. A} \textbf{79}, 062516 (2009) [reexamination]; Pachucki, K., Yerokhin, V.~A., \emph{Can. J. Phys.} \textbf{89}, 95 (2011) [arXiv:1011.1370, fine structure of He and light He-like ions]
step_1_compilations_identified.key_value_quoted: Reevaluated relativistic Bethe-logarithm term; obtained ν_01 = 29,616,946.2(1.6) kHz and ν_12 = 2,291,177.3(1.6) kHz; previously had 3-sigma discrepancy with Drake's calculation on ν_01
step_1_compilations_identified.convention_role: explicitly re-itemized the α^3(Zα)^2 multi-loop sector (specifically the relativistic Bethe-log) and identified that Drake's earlier value was off by ~3 standard deviations

step_2_convention_difference.summary: Pachucki and Yerokhin (PRA 79, 062516, 2009) explicitly reexamined helium fine structure and 'eliminated this [3σ] disagreement [vs Drake on ν_01] by reevaluating the relativistic Bethe logarithm term.' This is a direct convention exposure: Drake's 1990s-era itemization of the α^3(Zα)^2 sector was numerically incorrect (subsequently corrected in collaboration with Pachucki — 'eventually the results turned out to be consistent after Drake revised the sign,' per the literature retrieval). Specifically, the relativistic Bethe-log term sits inside the α^3(Zα)^2 multi-loop budget that the GeoVac framework does NOT capture (the framework's residual on P_0-P_1 is −0.014%, on P_0-P_2 is −0.20%, on P_1-P_2 is −2.62% from partial cancellation amplification — see V.C.4 He 2^3P autopsy).
step_2_convention_difference.structural_class: class-(i) inter-compilation itemization, same class as V.D.1, V.D.2, V.D.3

step_3_magnitude.Bethe_log_reevaluation: ~kHz on ν_01 = 29 GHz interval (Pachucki-Yerokhin's revised value 29,616,946.2 kHz vs Drake's older value disagreeing at the 3σ ~few kHz level)
step_3_magnitude.in_ppm_of_observable: ~0.1 ppm on ν_01 (kHz on GHz observable); but propagated into α determination this is the 27-31 ppb precision regime
step_3_magnitude.in_absolute_units: ~few kHz on the 29 GHz P_0-P_1 interval; or roughly ~1-2 kHz on the recent precision targets that determine α to ~30 ppb
step_3_magnitude.as_fraction_of_LS8a_budget: exactly inside the α^3(Zα)^2 multi-loop budget that the LS-8a wall identifies as the framework's structural limit; the convention difference between Drake-1990s and Pachucki-Yerokhin 2009 is INSIDE this same multi-loop budget, NOT below it
step_3_magnitude.as_fraction_of_alpha_determination: the helium 2^3P fine structure determines α to ~27-31 ppb; the Bethe-log reevaluation was the load-bearing fix for the discrepancy that earlier α-determination work had against muonium / electron-g-2 alternative determinations

candidates.step_4_verdict: CONFIRMED. Two well-defined compilations (Drake 1990s era, Pachucki-Yerokhin 2009/2010); explicit literature documentation of the disagreement and its resolution; magnitude (~kHz on 29 GHz observable; ~30 ppb on α determination) sits comfortably above the V.D entry threshold. Notably this also REFINES the §V.C.4 He 2^3P autopsy 'Class A: NEGATIVE — Drake and Pachucki-Yerokhin agree on leading-order Breit-Pauli itemization' — that statement was correct ONLY at the leading-order Breit-Pauli level, NOT at the α^3(Zα)^2 multi-loop level where the Bethe-log reevaluation lives.

step_5_VD_row_text.Observable: He 2^3P fine structure (29 GHz interval; α-determination scale)
step_5_VD_row_text.Convention_difference: Drake's original relativistic Bethe-log itemization was numerically incorrect; Pachucki-Yerokhin reevaluated and resolved a 3σ disagreement on ν_01
step_5_VD_row_text.Magnitude: ~kHz on 29 GHz observable; ~30 ppb on α determination
step_5_VD_row_text.Sprint_reference: V.D-prediction sprint, 2026-05-10; refines V.C.4 'Class A: NEGATIVE' to be 'Class A: NEGATIVE at LO Breit-Pauli, POSITIVE at α^3(Zα)^2 multi-loop level'

candidates.VC4_refinement_required: §V.C.4 He 2^3P autopsy currently states 'Class A (literature convention mismatch): NEGATIVE — Drake (1971) and Pachucki--Yerokhin (2010) agree on leading-order Breit-Pauli itemization.' This sprint shows the agreement is on LO Breit-Pauli ONLY, NOT at the α^3(Zα)^2 multi-loop level (specifically the relativistic Bethe-log term). §V.C.4 needs a refinement note added cross-referencing V.D.6.

pattern_C_status_after.candidate_C_class_assignment: Lamb-class precision spectroscopy (NOT strictly HFS); pattern broadens slightly from 'HFS / muonic-Lamb' to 'precision-spectroscopy class observables that decompose into many sub-leading Layer-2 channels'
pattern_C_status_after.falsifier_status: next sprint should attempt §V.D entry on a non-precision-spectroscopy observable (e.g., heavy-atom HFS at coarse experimental precision, or molecular vibrational spectroscopy). If the framework cannot find a class-(i) split there, Pattern C tightens further.

paper_34.label: sec:conv_d_polarizability
paper_34.content_summary: Friar-Payne 2005 vs Pachucki-Yerokhin 2010+ on D HFS polarizability sub-component

paper_34.label: sec:conv_he_bethe_log
paper_34.content_summary: Drake 1990s vs Pachucki-Yerokhin 2009 on He 2^3P relativistic Bethe-log reevaluation
extend cross-pattern paragraph to include V.D.5 (class-i) and V.D.6 (class-i, Lamb-class extension); pattern broadens slightly
refine 'Class A: NEGATIVE' statement to 'Class A: NEGATIVE at LO Breit-Pauli, POSITIVE at α^3(Zα)^2 multi-loop level via the relativistic Bethe-log reevaluation; cross-ref V.D.6'

follow_ups_flagged.specific_targets: Karshenboim 2005 Phys.Rep.422 §6.2-6.5 vs CMY 2000 PRL 82,311 + PRA 59,4316 vs Adkins-Czarnecki 2014 PRL 112,120407 — does Karshenboim's review separate the one-photon annihilation amplitude from radiative QED corrections in a different scheme than CMY's analytic decomposition?
follow_ups_flagged.if_negative: confirms Ps HFS literature has converged on a single itemization

no_redundancy_with_existing_VD_entries.V.D.1_vs_V.D.5: V.D.1 is the global D HFS Eides-vs-PY itemization mismatch; V.D.5 refines V.D.1 to the polarizability-specific sub-component (Friar-Payne 2005 vs PY 2010+). They are NOT duplicates: V.D.1 is broader (Eides Tab 7.3 budget vs PY budget); V.D.5 is finer (specific polarizability term, Friar-Payne 1979/2005 vs PY 2010+).
no_redundancy_with_existing_VD_entries.V.D.2_vs_V.D.6: V.D.2 is muonic H Lamb shift one-loop VP itemization (Antognini 2013 vs Krauth 2017); V.D.6 is electronic He 2^3P fine structure α^3(Zα)^2 multi-loop (Drake 1990s vs Pachucki-Yerokhin 2009). Different observables, different orders, different compilations — no redundancy.

honest_negatives: Candidate B (Ps 1S HFS annihilation channel) was a real INCONCLUSIVE — sprint scope was insufficient to resolve. Web search recovered three citations but no clear A-vs-B itemization difference. Honest negative; flagged for deeper follow-up sprint.

Pattern C has broadened — Candidate C is Lamb-class fine structure, NOT strictly HFS. The pattern is now best described as 'HFS or precision-spectroscopy with multi-component Layer-2 decomposition' rather than 'HFS only.' This is a sharpening, not a falsification.

Critical caveat for V.D.5: significant overlap with V.D.1. Could be argued as a refinement-of-V.D.1 rather than a new entry; we kept it as V.D.5 per protocol since the convention-axis differs (Friar-Payne vs PY, vs the V.D.1 Eides vs PY axis). Future audit may merge.

data_provenance.confidence_in_verdicts: HIGH for A, HIGH for C, MODERATE for B-INCONCLUSIVE; verdicts based on natural-language search summaries that quote specific numerical values; PDF-level deep dive would tighten quantitative claims for V.D.5 magnitude
